title: ericameria watsonii
intro: Ericameria watsonii, also known as Watson's greasewood, is a shrub species in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Ericameria watsonii include relief from skin irritations and wounds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, with herbal preparations typically involving infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and stems.
